calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-05-2026 Vacated

Petition For Instructions Regarding Interpretation Of Trust Terms
Off Calendar Aug-05-2025 Continued To Feb-02-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Walter Cook for Randall A. Boris, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Jonathan Fuller for Gina Grasso, Objector (via Zoom).

Discussion was held regarding trial setting, both parties agree to have their matter set for trial.

The matters are set for trial in the Department of the Presiding Judge (206) on February 2, 2026 at 9:30 a.m., (order for the same signed in open court).

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Conference
Off Calendar Aug-05-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Walter Cook for Randall A. Boris, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Jonathan Fuller for Gina Grasso, Objector (via Zoom).

Discussion was held regarding trial setting, both parties agree to have their matter set for trial.

The matters are set for trial in the Department of the Presiding Judge (206) on February 2, 2026 at 9:30 a.m., (order for the same signed in open court).

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions Regarding Interpretation Of Trust Terms
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Continued To Aug-05-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Walter Cook for Randall A. Boris, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Jonathan Fuller for Gina Grasso, Objector (via Zoom).

Mr. Cook reported that the parties had made progress in discovery and were scheduled to conduct a significant document inspection that week. He requested that the matter either be set for trial or continued for further trial setting. He estimated a three- to five-day trial, citing anticipated testimony from multiple witnesses.

Mr. Fuller opposed immediate trial setting and argued that discovery should be bifurcated to first address the validity of the second amended trust, which he characterized as the threshold issue. He raised concerns that much of the discovery conducted by Petitioner focused on estate property rather than the authenticity of the trust document. The Court responded that it is not the Court's role to restrict discovery strategy but encouraged the parties to meet and confer to resolve disputes without motion practice.

The Court set the matter for a case management conference on August 5, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to assess the status of discovery and determine whether the case is ready for trial. The Court continued the petition to be heard on the same date.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Instructions Regarding Interpretation Of Trust Terms
Off Calendar Mar-18-2025 Continued To May-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions Regarding Interpretation Of Trust Terms
Off Calendar Jan-07-2025 Continued To Mar-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions Regarding Interpretation Of Trust Terms
Off Calendar Nov-12-2024 Continued To Jan-07-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions Regarding Interpretation Of Trust Terms
Off Calendar Aug-13-2024 Continued To Nov-12-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Walter Cook appeared by zoom for and with Randall Boris (petitioner) by zoom.
Andrew Gibaut appeared by zoom for and with Gina Grasso (objector) by zoom.

The court informed Mr. Cook that it does not have a proposed order from him. Mr. Cook stated that he will provide one, and that he anticipates doing some discovery and negotiations, and perhaps mediation. Mr. Gibaut agrees that this is where they are heading at this point. Discussion was held regarding pro bono mediation and the possibility of getting a referral from the court at a later time. The Petition for Instructions Regarding Interpretation of Trust Terms is continued to November 12, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Aguerre. Not reported.
